texts.full_text: by a board of directors which is appointed by the church. In 2015, the store opened its first satellite location in Center City Allentown as part of the Two City Center complex. The location was closed in May 2017 to refocus on the Bethlehem location and remaining stock was moved there. In April 2018, nearby Moravian College announced the acquisition of the shop. The historic bookstore would become the central student bookstore for the college. Employee Leo Atkinson created a petition to the college to reconsider their decision. Features The Moravian Book Shop features a book section that includes indie best sellers, trade books, and select books highlighting the history of Moravians, Bethlehem, Bethlehem Steel, the Lehigh Valley and Pennsylvania. In addition, the Moravian Book Shop sells and buys back student textbooks. The Book Shop also stocks Moravian College apparel and gift items, reference books, stationery and art supplies, greeting cards, candy, and health and beauty aids. In addition, the inventory also includes traditional Moravian- and Bethlehem-themed gifts and College faculty- and alumni-authored books. The store also hosts weekly reading and discussion groups and monthly events with authors. Moravian Stars The store specializes in the sale of hundreds of Moravian Stars, also called Advent Stars, which were first produced in 19th-century Germany. The 26-point stars are produced in many styles and fabricated in various materials. These will continue to be produced with the change of ownership. References External links Official website Photos

texts.full_text: nts might be regarding relationships and meeting partners online. Here are a few suggestions to consider as part of handling the introduction: 1) Communicate Openly with Your Parents: Before introducing Jack, have an open conversation about your relationship status without immediately bringing up specifics that may cause alarm. Let them know you've met someone important but gently address their concerns by highlighting how much thought and effort went into getting to understand him both online before meeting in person. 2) Highlight Positive Aspects of Your Relationship: Emphasize the positive qualities Jack brings, such as his support for your education goals or any other admirable traits that align with what they value. This can help shift their focus from potential negatives like age difference and how you met to more constructive thoughts about compatibility based on values. 3) Ease Into Introductions Gradually: You could start by inviting Jack over when family isn't around initially, allowing them time for casual interaction where your parents see him as just another friend rather than an immediate romantic interest. This gradual approach might make the eventual introduction smoother once they feel more comfortable with who he is beyond initial reservations. 4) Prepare to Address Concerns: Be ready and willing not only talk about Jack's background but also discuss common fears related meeting people online (e.g., safety, authenticity). The event starts at 6 and is free and open to the general public. KGB Bar is on 85 East 4th Street (near 2nd Avenue) New York, New York 10003 * * * Burning Furiously Beautiful: The True Story of Jack Kerouac’s “On the Road” is a fascinating exploration of the real-life characters and events that inspired one of the most iconic novels in American literature. In this excerpt, author Stephanie Nikolopoulos delves into Kerouac's Greek connection through his friendship with Sebastian Sampas from Lowell. The evening promises to be a captivating celebration of diverse literary voices and artistic expressions. From poetry readings to musical performances, it showcases the talent and creativity within the community. Join Stephanie and the other renowned writers for an unforgettable experience at KGB Bar. Remember, the event is free and open to all, starting from 6 p.m., with a remarkable lineup that includes both established authors and emerging artists: - Professors: Wang Ping, Miguel Algarin, Nancy Mercado, Carlos Manuel Rivera, Julie Patton, Everton Sylvester - Poets & Musicians: Carl Hancock Rux, Jeff Wright, Brian Omni Dillon, Ronnie Norpel, Susan Yung, Kate Levin, Sarah Sarai, Stephanie Nikolopoulos (with acoustic guitar and harmonicas) - Special Performance: Cornelius Eady

I have to go by my experience.....most of my recruits are people who were not looking but were open-minded (and, yes, occasionally resistant) or at the least, I was able to 'convert' them from 'go away' to staying on the phone and letting me finish/call back with a description. I'm not fighting you on this- what works for you is great....it just seems contrary to the nature of Executive Search to ignore those who put out red flags....in my training, Keith, they are all waving red flags. If I think this out....wondering if I would be glad to have 'green flags' to pull from first....I would still want to know who all the red flags are since in the end, I still need to call them, get to know them so they all must go into my DB so I can call them back someday when I have the 'just right' opportunity for them. If I took this conversation back to my manager and explained this all to him....bringing him forward from the days of inventing the wheel and fire....he'd still say I need to know all of those who are qualified. texts.full_text: to learn a tilt and balance wheelchair skill. Often service professionals working with individuals who use wheelchairs find themselves inadequately prepared to give hands-on or verbal assistance in wheelchair maneuverability skills. The purpose of this study was to examine the effectiveness of two methods of teaching a tilt and balance wheelchair skill, evaluated by using an obstacle course. Subjects were 30 volunteers, had their first experience with wheelchairs. The participants underwent training sessions where they learned how to use both manual (self-propelled) and power-assisted chairs in order to improve the quality of life for individuals who are unable or have difficulty walking due to physical limitations. The study aimed at comparing two different teaching methods: traditional verbal instruction combined with demonstration, versus a hands-on approach that involves direct assistance from an instructor. During each training session lasting approximately one hour and fifteen minutes long (1 15), participants were divided into three groups of ten individuals per group based on their learning preference style. Group A received only the verbal instructions along with visual demonstrations while Groups B & C had instructors physically assist them in maneuvering through various obstacles such as ramps, curbs or narrow pathways. The study concluded that both methods have advantages and disadvantages depending upon individual preferences for instructional styles; however it was found more effective to combine these two approaches together. This combination of verbal instructions combined with hands-on assistance proved most beneficial when learning how to tilt & balance a wheelchair as participants were able to understand the concepts better while also being physically guided through each skill set. The results showed that Group B, who received direct physical instruction in addition to traditional methods had significantly higher success rates compared to other groups. This suggests there is value added from combining different teaching methodologies and tailoring them according to individual learning preferences which can lead towards more effective training programs for service professionals working with wheelchair

texts.full_text: 3 Weston decided to re-train as a doctor and attended medical school in London from 1993. She qualified as a doctor in 2000 and became a member of the Royal College of Surgeons in 2003. Direct Red and Dirty Work In February 2009, Weston's book Direct Red: A Surgeon's Story, a set of 14 short stories based on her experiences as a surgeon, was published. The book received critical acclaim for its honest portrayal of the medical profession and was well-received by readers. Weston followed up this success with another collection of essays titled Dirty Work: Searching for Treasure in the Muck of Life, which was published in 2017. This book explores various aspects of life outside the conventional career path, offering insights into how unconventional jobs can lead to personal growth and fulfillment.

texts.full_text: ( column1 datatype PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, column2 datatype NOT NULL, column3 datatype NOT NULL, CONSTRAINT fk_keyname FOREIGN KEY (colmn3) REFERENCES otherTable (column3) ); CREATE TABLE table2 ( column1 datatype PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, column2 datatype NOT NULL, column3 datatype NOT NULL, CONSTRAINT fk_keyname2 FOREIGN KEY (column3) REFERENCES table1 (column1) ); However, as you've noted correctly in your question, this will not work because `otherTable` does not exist when the first table is being created. To solve this issue and create two tables with mutual foreign key references, follow these steps: ### Step 1: Create both Tables Without Foreign Key Constraints First, define each of the tables without specifying any foreign keys. ```sql CREATE TABLE table1 ( column1 datatype PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, column2 datatype NOT NULL, column3 datatype -- No FOREIGN KEY constraint here yet ); CREATE TABLE table2 ( column1 datatype PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, column2 datatype NOT NULL, column3 datatype -- No FOREIGN KEY constraint here yet ); ``` ### Step 2: Add Foreign Key Constraints After Both Tables Are Created Once both tables are created, you can add the foreign key constraints using `ALTER TABLE`. ```sql -- Adding FK to table1 referencing a primary/unique key in table2 (column3 -> column1) ALTER TABLE table1 ADD CONSTRAINT fk_table1_to_table2 FOREIGN KEY (column3) REFERENCES table2(column1); -- Adding FK to table2 referencing the unique constraint or PK of table1 ALTER TABLE table2 ADD CONSTRAINT fk_table2_to_table1 FOREIGN KEY (column3) REFERENCES table1(column1); ``` ### Important Considerations - Circular Dependencies: Be cautious with circular dependencies as they can complicate data management, especially when it comes to inserting and deleting rows. You might need a `DEFERRABLE` constraint or use specific insert/update/delete strategies. If you encounter issues due to the mutual dependency during inserts/updates/deletes (e.g., "cannot update table referenced in foreign key"), consider making one of the constraints deferrable:

texts.full_text: Skip to primary navigation Skip to main content artful words crafting your written expression Services Editing Writing Proofreading Advice Articles The Art of Editing Craft of Writing Wonderful Words Grammar, etc. About Contact Is perfection in editing possible? No, there's more to the story than that. Recently, I had an unpleasant experience with mistakes found in a document I edited and sent back to a client. By time I discovered them it was too late for any remedy as they were already sent to production leaving me embarrassed apologetic but also disappointing myself since i regard all my work seriously strive ensure its free of errors possible A list reasons why this oversight occurred sound like excuses poor ones at that. Instead, I'd offer some helpful reminders for any writer: Edit your own writing last; Fatigue can lead to overlooking mistakes Recognize when you need a fresh set eyes Review work aloud Listen carefully as you read Allow time between drafting and finalizing Give yourself space distance from the text Take breaks, step away Let someone else review before publishing These reminders serve not only myself but all writers.

Here's a breakdown of key components in hydraulic devices: 1. Hydraulic Fluid: This is the liquid that transmits power throughout the system. It is typically an fluid that is resistant to heat, pressure, and oxidation. 2. Reservoir: This is the container that holds the hydraulic fluid when it's not in use. It also provides a place for the fluid to expand when it heats up. 3. Pump: The pump is responsible for creating the flow and pressure in the hydraulic system. It forces the fluid through the system, causing movement in the hydraulic motors and cylinders. 4. Control Valves: These valves regulate the flow of hydraulic fluid to different parts of the system. They can be manually controlled or automated based on the system's requirements. 5. Hydraulic Cylinders: These are linear actuators that use hydraulic pressure to create linear motion. They are often used in lifting and lowering applications. 6. Hydraulic Motors: These convert hydraulic pressure into rotary motion. They are used in applications where rotational movement is required. 7. Hydraulic Pipes and Hoses: These carry the hydraulic fluid from the pump to the various components of the system. They must be designed to withstand high pressures. 8. Filters: These are used to remove contaminants from the hydraulic fluid, ensuring the smooth operation of the system. 9. texts.full_text: lip right through the building at top speed, sometimes flipping itself into the air when it emerges. It sounds like you're encountering issues with physics collision detection in Unity as your vehicle's velocity increases. Here are a few suggestions to help resolve these unexpected behaviors: 1. Increase Collision Detection: By default, colliders might not be checked for collisions every frame at high speeds due to performance optimizations (especially if they have the "Discrete" setting). Change this by selecting both your car and building objects in Unity Editor: - Go into their respective Rigidbody components. - Set `Collision detection` from Discrete to Continuous or Continous Speculative. This ensures that physics engine checks for collisions more frequently, reducing tunneling issues. 2. Adjust Collider Size: Ensure the colliders on both your car and building are appropriately sized relative to each other. Sometimes small adjustments can make a big difference in how objects interact during high-speed impacts. 3. Optimize Rigidbody Settings: - Make sure that `Interpolate` is turned off if you're experiencing jittery or unstable movement, as interpolation helps smooth out motion but might cause issues with collision detection at very fast speeds. 4. Check for Mesh Issues: If your building has a complex mesh and the collider does not match it well (e.g., using too simple of a convex hull), this can lead to unexpected behaviors during collisions. - Use `Mesh Collider` components instead, or refine existing colliders better fit their respective objects. 5. Adjust Physics Material Properties: Sometimes tweaking friction values on your physics materials attached to the car and building's surfaces might help with stability post-collision (e.g., preventing flipping). 6. Increase Fixed Timestep: - Go into `Edit > Project Settings` in Unity, then select `Time`. - Increase or fine-tune both 'Fixed timestep' values under Physics settings for more accurate physics calculations. 7. texts.full_text: of "harm" needs clarification. In a medical context, one could interpret 'harm' as physical discomfort experienced by the patient (pain), but does it also include potential long-term effects from chronic diseases? Does administering painkillers that shorten life span or reduce the quality of end-of-life care count towards causing "harm"? Secondly, what if a physician administers a high dose without adequate justification—knowingly intending to accelerate death rather than simply providing symptomatic relief. Is this act still covered by the doctrine? The legal boundaries between legitimate pain management and intentional euthanasia are blurry. Thirdly, laws on doctor-assisted suicide or euthanasia vary greatly around the world; while some countries like the Netherlands have a strictly regulated framework for assisted dying under certain conditions (like incurable diseases), other jurisdictions might not recognize such practices at all. Therefore, the legal standing of administering painkillers with an intent to hasten death can depend significantly on jurisdiction. Fourthly, considering moral and ethical dimensions: is it ethically permissible or obligatory for a physician to end suffering even if doing so requires administering doses that could accelerate the patient's demise? These questions challenge not only legal but also medical ethics norms. texts.full_text: a complete webpage How can I download a complete webpage through terminal. Providing address of a webpage should automatically indicate the suTo download a complete webpage (including all linked resources like images, CSS, JavaScript, etc.) via the terminal, you can use the `wget` or `curl` command. Here's a breakdown of the most common approach using `wget`: --- ### Using `wget` to Download a Complete Webpage ```bash wget --recursive \ --no-parent \ --page-requisites \ --convert-links \ --adjust-extension \ --user-agent="Mozilla" \ [URL] ``` #### Explanation of Flags: - `--recursive`: Enables recursive downloading (follows links to download subpages). - `--no-parent`: Avoids ascending to the parent directory (prevents over-downloading). - `--page-requisites`: Downloads all required resources (images, CSS, JS, etc.) for the page to Display properly. - `--convert-links`: Adjusts links in downloaded files to work offline. - `--adjust-extension`: Adds proper file extensions (e.g., `.html` for HTML files). - `--user-agent="Mozilla"`: Mimics a browser to avoid being blocked by servers. #### Example: ```bash wget --recursive --no-parent --page-requisites --convert-links --adjust-extension --user-agent="Mozilla" https://example.com ``` --- ### Alternative: Using `curl` (Limited) `curl` is better for single-page downloads. For full site mirroring, `wget` is preferred. However, for a single page: ```bash curl -O [URL] ``` --- ### Notes: - Respect `robots.txt`: By default, `wget` respects the site’s `robots.txt` file. Use `--no-robots` to bypass this (not recommended for public sites). - Bandwidth/Rate Limiting: Add `--limit-rate=200k` to limit download speed (adjust as needed). - Random Delays: Use `--random-wait` to avoid triggering server rate limits. --- ### Output Directory The files will be saved in a directory named after t

texts.full_text: and Wethersfield churches were affected by disputes over questions of church order, including a dispute over the eligibility requirements for infant baptism. Although a council met in Hartford to seek a solution to these differences in June 1656, the Hartford minister and his church refused to abide by the council's recommendations, and religious dissension persisted in the troubled colony. Despite the fact that he had been named Connecticut's First Assistant following his year as governor, Webster in 1659 joined with those members of the Hartford church who had decided to unite with other dissidents and establish a new congregation in Hadley, Massachusetts. After he settled in Hadley, Webster was in May 1660 "commissioned with magistratticall power for the year ensuing" by the Massachusetts General Court, but he died on April 5, 1661, before his term had expired.
